messages.text_original:
Фенікс сьогодні гуляти не вийде... а може і завтра не вийде, якщо наших петабайтів вистачить
—
Phoenix won’t be going out for a walk today… and maybe not tomorrow either, if our petabytes last.
messages.text_en: Phoenix won’t be able to go for a walk today… and maybe not even tomorrow, if our petabytes last until then.

messages.text_original:
Нагадаємо, що Фенікс це мобільний оператор, який працює на тимчасово окупованій частині Донеччини та, за заявами окупаційної адміністрації, обслуговує понад 750 000 верифікованих абонентів і фактично має монопольне становище. 

Він є інфраструктурно незамінним, оскільки через нього забезпечується зв’язок населення, робота окупаційних структур, а з великою ймовірністю також військових командних пунктів РФ, особливо в умовах обмеженої або відсутньої доступності Starlink.
—
To recall, Phoenix is a mobile operator operating in the temporarily occupied part of Donetsk region and, according to claims by the occupation administration, serves more than 750,000 verified subscribers, effectively holding a monopoly position. 

It is infrastructure-critical, as it supports civilian communications, the functioning of occupation authorities, and with high probability also Russian military command posts, especially in conditions of limited or unavailable Starlink access.
messages.text_en:
Let’s remember that Phoenix is a mobile operator that operates in the temporarily occupied part of Donetsk region. According to claims made by the occupation administration, it serves more than 750,000 verified subscribers. In other words, it effectively holds a monopoly position in this area.

It’s extremely important for infrastructure purposes, as it provides communication services for civilians, supports the functioning of occupation authorities, and most likely also Russian military command posts. This is especially true under conditions where Starlink access is limited or unavailable.

messages.text_original:
Все пишуть про нас і пишуть. Селебріті да і тільки.

За останній тиждень кількість нових людей які приєднались до наших операцій збільшилась втричі. 

Дякуємо за довіру. друзі! No pasaran!
—
Everyone keeps writing about us, and writing. Celebrities, nothing else.

Over the past week, the number of new people who joined our operations has tripled.

Thank you for the trust, friends! No pasarán!
messages.text_en:
Everyone writes about us, and continues to do so. Celebrities… nothing else. 

In the past week alone, the number of new people who joined our programs has tripled. 

Thank you for your trust, friends! No pasarán!

messages.text_original:
Як думаєте вже час видумати гучну назву нашій з вами операції по найбільшому послідовному падінню інтернет провайдерів в історії? 

 Пишіть у чат . Проведемо голосування серед найкращих варіантів
—
Do you think it’s already time to come up with a loud, catchy name for our operation involving the largest sequential collapse of internet providers in history?

 Write your suggestions in the chat . We’ll hold a vote among the best options.
messages.text_en:
What do you think is the right time to come up with a catchy name for our operation, which involves the largest sequential collapse of internet providers in history?

Please write your suggestions in the chat. We’ll conduct a vote among the best options.

messages.text_original:
Увага! Є питання
—
Attention! There is a question
messages.text_en: Attention! There is a question.

messages.text_original:
Вай вай. Звідки тільки беруться ці "нєдображелатєлі"?
—
Wow, wow. Where do all these ‘haters’ keep coming from?
messages.text_en: Wow. Where on earth do all these “haters” come from?

messages.text_original:
Продовжуються і будуть продовжуватись поки війська агресора будуть користуватися послугами цивільних провайдерів
—
They are continuing and will continue as long as the aggressor’s troops use the services of civilian providers
messages.text_en: They will continue to do so, and will continue to do so as long as the aggressor’s troops use the services of civilian providers.

messages.text_original:
США решили перебросить авианосец USS Gerald R. Ford и сопровождающие его корабли из Карибского моря в Персидский залив на фоне напряженности в отношениях с Ираном.
Согласно приказу, полученному 12 февраля, USS Gerald R. Ford присоединится к авианосной ударной группе во главе с USS Abraham Lincoln, ранее направленной на Ближний Восток.

Авианосная группа USS Gerald R. Ford участвовала в операции по захвату президента Венесуэлы Николас Мадуро в Каракасе 3 января.
messages.text_en: The United States has decided to transfer the aircraft carrier USS Gerald R. Ford, along with its accompanying ships, from the Caribbean Sea to the Persian Gulf, amid tensions with Iran. According to an order received on February 12th, the USS Gerald R. Ford will join a naval strike group led by the USS Abraham Lincoln, which was previously sent to the Middle East. The USS Gerald R. Ford’s aircraft carrier group participated in the operation to capture Venezuelan President Nicolas Maduro in Caracas on January 3rd.

🇺🇦 🇨🇳 The head of the Ukrainian Ministry of Foreign Affairs, Sybiga, after meeting with his Chinese counterpart, Wang Yi:  
– China supports the territorial integrity of Ukraine.  
– China will provide Ukraine with a package of energy assistance.

🇩🇪 German Chancellor Merz:  
– I have started confidential negotiations with French President Emmanuel Macron regarding European nuclear deterrence.  
– We will not allow the existence of zones with different levels of security in Europe.  
– Discussions are underway with Paris regarding the possibility of expanding France’s nuclear “shield” to protect EU countries, including Germany.

messages.text_original:
«Я публично предупреждаю вас, что при нынешних тенденциях следующая мировая война неизбежна», — заявил французский военачальник Фердинанд Фош. Это был 1921 год, и Фош, главнокомандующий союзными армиями во время Первой мировой войны, бил тревогу в своей речи из Нью-Йорка. Его опасения были просты. После разгрома Германии союзные державы заставили её разоружиться Версальским договором. Но всего через пару лет они перестали выполнять условия своей победы. Берлин, предупреждал Фош, таким образом, мог и будет восстанавливать свою армию. «Если союзники продолжат своё нынешнее безразличие… Германия непременно снова поднимется в вооружённом восстании».

Комментарии Фоша оказались пророческими. К концу 1930-х годов Германия действительно восстановила свою армию. Она захватила Австрию, затем Чехословакию, а затем Польшу, что спровоцировало Вторую мировую войну. После очередного поражения союзники стали более внимательно относиться к управлению страной. Они оккупировали и разделили её, расформировали вооруженные силы и в значительной степени упразднили оборонную промышленность. Когда Соединенные Штаты и Советский Союз позволили Западной и Восточной Германии, соответственно, восстановить свои вооруженные силы, это происходило только под строгим контролем. Когда же они разрешили объединение двух частей, Германии пришлось ограничить численность своих вооруженных сил. Тем не менее, премьер-министр Великобритании Маргарет Тэтчер выступала против воссоединения, опасаясь, что это приведет к созданию опасно могущественной страны. Более крупная Германия, предупреждала она в 1989 году, «подорвет стабильность всей международной ситуации и может поставить под угрозу нашу безопасность».

Сегодня опасения Фоша и Тэтчер кажутся пережитками прошлого. В последние десятилетия Европа переживала один кризис за другим — прежде всего, российскую агрессию против Украины — и европейские чиновники беспокоились не о том, что Берлин может стать слишком сильным, а о том, что он слишком слаб. «Я боюсь немецкой мощи меньше, чем бездействия Германии», — заявил Радослав Сикорский, министр иностранных дел Польши, в 2011 году во время европейского финансового кризиса. Это было примечательное заявление для польского чиновника, учитывая, что Варшава традиционно была одним из правительств, наиболее обеспокоенных немецкой мощью. Он далеко не одинок: немецкая армия должна «тратить больше и производить больше», заявил генеральный секретарь НАТО Марк Рютте в 2024 году.

Теперь эти лидеры получают то, чего хотели. После многих задержек немецкое «Цайтенвенде» — обещание Германии стать одним из лидеров обороны Европы к 2022 году — наконец-то становится реальностью. В 2025 году Германия потратила на оборону больше, чем любая другая европейская страна в абсолютном выражении. Сегодня её военный бюджет занимает четвёртое место в мире, сразу после России. Ожидается, что ежегодные военные расходы достигнут 189 миллиардов долларов в 2029 году, что более чем в три раза превышает показатель 2022 года. Германия даже рассматривает возможность возвращения к обязательной воинской повинности, если её армия, Бундесвер, не сможет привлечь достаточное количество добровольцев. Если страна продолжит следовать этому курсу, она снова станет великой военной державой к 2030 году.

(«Следующий гегемон Европы», Foreign Affairs)
messages.text_en:
“I publicly warn you that, given the current trends, another world war is inevitable,” said French military leader Ferdinand Foch. It was the year 1921, and Foch, who was the commander-in-chief of the Allied forces during World War I, expressed his concerns in a speech from New York. His fears were simple: after being defeated by Germany, the Allied powers forced Germany to disarm through the Treaty of Versailles. But just a few years later, they began to ignore the terms of their victory. Berlin could, and would, rebuild its army again. “If the Allies continue to be indifferent… Germany will definitely rise up in an armed rebellion again.”

Foch’s predictions turned out to be prophetic. By the end of the 1930s, Germany had indeed rebuilt its army. It captured Austria, then Czechoslovakia, and finally Poland. This led to World War II. After another defeat, the Allies became more cautious about managing the country. They occupied and divided it up, disbanded its armed forces, and largely eliminated its defense industry. When the United States and the Soviet Union allowed West Germany and East Germany to rebuild their armies, they did so under strict control. When they allowed the two parts to merge, Germany had to limit its military forces. However, British Prime Minister Margaret Thatcher opposed the reunification, fearing that it would lead to the creation of a dangerously powerful country. She warned in 1989 that a larger Germany could undermine global stability and threaten our security.

Today, Foch’s and Thatcher’s concerns seem like things of the past. In recent decades, Europe has experienced one crisis after another—especially Russia’s aggression against Ukraine. European officials are not worried about Berlin becoming too powerful, but rather about it becoming too weak. “I fear German power less than Germany’s inaction,” said Polish Foreign Minister Radosław Sikorski during the European financial crisis in 2011. This was a notable statement from a Polish official, considering Warsaw has always been one of the governments most concerned about German power. He wasn’t alone: NATO Secretary-General Jean-Claude Juncker said in 2024 that the German army should “spend more and produce more.”

Now, these leaders have achieved what they wanted. After many delays, Germany’s “Zeitgenossenschaft” – the promise that Germany would become one of Europe’s leading defense powers by 2022 – is finally becoming a reality. In 2025, Germany spent more on defense than any other European country. Its military budget now ranks fourth in the world, after Russia. It is expected that military spending will reach $189 billion by 2029, more than three times the level in 2022. Germany even considers returning to mandatory military service if its army cannot recruit enough volunteers. If the country continues along this path, it will once again become a powerful military power by 2030.

(“The Next Hegemony of Europe”, Foreign Affairs)

messages.text_original:
🇺🇦 Зеленский:

Встретился с наследным принцем Ирана в изгнании Резой Пехлеви в Мюнхене. Украина поддерживает иранский народ в его борьбе за своё будущее и сочувствует всем жертвам иранского режима. 

Во время беседы уделили внимание ситуации в Иране, направлениям, в которых люди Ирана нуждаются в поддержке. Обсудили важность усиления санкций против иранского режима и любых других диктаторских режимов. 

Осудили сотрудничество между Россией и Ираном, в частности поставки иранским режимом "шахедов" в Россию и передачу лицензий на соответствующее производство. Такое партнёрство представляет реальную угрозу не только для Украины, но и для всего региона. 

Спасибо принцу Ирана за поддержку территориальной целостности Украины. Важно прилагать максимум усилий для защиты человеческих жизней. Украина готова помочь со своей стороны.
messages.text_en:
🇺🇦 Zelenskyy:  
I met with the exiled heir to the throne of Iran, Reza Pahlavi, in Munich. Ukraine supports the Iranian people in their struggle for their future. We also sympathize with all those who have suffered under the Iranian regime.  

During our conversation, we discussed the situation in Iran and the areas where the Iranian people need support. We emphasized the importance of strengthening sanctions against the Iranian regime and any other dictatorial regimes.  

We condemned any cooperation between Russia and Iran. In particular, we criticized Iran’s efforts to supply weapons to Russia and provide licenses for related production activities. Such cooperation poses a real threat not only to Ukraine, but also to the entire region.  

Thank you, Prince of Iran, for your support for Ukraine’s territorial integrity. It’s important to make every effort to protect human lives. Ukraine is ready to help in whatever way it can.

messages.text_original:
Военный аналитик Датского королевского оборонного колледжа Андерс Нильсен - про недавние раунды переговоров между США, РФ и Украиной: 

• У стороннего наблюдателя может сложится ложное впечатление, что переговоры идут хорошо: все стороны постоянно заявляют, что достигли большого прогресса. Разумеется, это не так — позиции украинцев и русских практически не изменились. Более того, в РФ и Украине нет свидетельств подготовки общественности и армии к тому, что придется пойти на компромиссы. Как и раньше, как украинцы, так и русские стараются убедить Трампа, что именно другая сторона является препятствием к миру.

• Сейчас говорят относительно двух разных документов: один касается мира между Украиной и РФ, а второй — относительно гарантий безопасности между Украиной, Европой и США. Заявляется, что оба документа почти готовы. В первом из них требуется лишь решить вопрос Донбасса, так как русские по-прежнему требуют полного контроля над ним. Во втором же очередь за ратификацией документа западными странами.

• Разделение соглашений на два явно является ходом украинцев не допустить права вето русских на оказание помощи странами Запада. Не стоит забывать, что вопрос гарантий безопасности является ключевым для Украины. Цель русских полностью подчинить себе всю страну, и они уже неоднократно требовали, что тоже должны стать "гарантами безопасности" с правом блокировать чужую помощь. Разделив вопросы на два документа, украинцы просто пытаются выбросить русских из обсуждений ключевого вопроса.

• В свою очередь, русские ссылаются на некое "понимание, достигнутое на саммите в Анкоридже". Не понятно, что они имеют ввиду: Трамп явно был разочарован тем саммитом. Фактически, русские пытаются вернутся назад к плану из 28-ми пунктов, появившегося после Анкориджа, и который уже был отвергнут.

• Вопрос территорий Донбасса, находящихся под украинским контролем — никогда не будет решен на этих переговорах. Вопрос тут не в том, какие населенные пункты или население могут перейти под российский контроль. Вопрос в линиях укреплений в Донецкой области, которые сейчас являются главной гарантией безопасности Украины. Русские, возможно, потеряют полмиллиона солдат, пока смогут захватить эти укрепления. Сдача этих укреплений без боя равнозначна самоубийству.

• Таким образом, от текущих переговоров не стоит ожидать каких-либо прорывов. Поводов для оптимизма, основанных на заявлениях, что процесс идет конструктивно, на самом деле нет. Война продолжится до тех пор, пока одна из сторон не ослабнет настолько, что согласится на условия другой.
messages.text_en:
Anders Nielsen, a military analyst at the Royal Defense College of Denmark, commented on the recent negotiations between the United States, Russia, and Ukraine:

• A foreign observer might get the wrong impression that the negotiations are going well. All parties constantly claim to have made significant progress. Of course, this isn’t true. The positions of Ukrainians and Russians haven’t changed at all. Moreover, there is no sign that either Russia or Ukraine is preparing the public and its military for any kind of compromise. Both sides continue to try to convince Trump that it’s the other side that is preventing peace.

• Currently, two different documents are being discussed: one relates to peace between Ukraine and Russia, while the other concerns security guarantees between Ukraine, Europe, and the United States. It seems that both documents are almost ready for approval. In the first document, only the Donbas region needs to be resolved, as Russians still want full control over it. In the second document, Western countries still need to ratify it.

• Dividing the agreements into two separate documents is clearly a way for Ukrainians to avoid giving Russia any veto power over Western assistance. It’s important to remember that security guarantees are crucial for Ukraine. The goal of Russians is to control the entire country. They have repeatedly demanded that they also become “guarantors of security,” with the right to block foreign aid. By dividing the issues into two documents, Ukrainians are trying to get Russia out of the discussion about these key issues.

• On the other hand, Russians refer to some “understanding reached at the summit in Anchorage.” It’s unclear what they mean by this. Trump was clearly disappointed with that summit. In fact, Russians are trying to return to the 28-point plan that emerged after Anchorage, but that plan has been rejected.

• The issue of Donbas region under Ukrainian control will never be resolved in these negotiations. The real issue isn’t which towns or people can be taken over by Russia. The real issue is the positions of the fortifications in Donetsk province, which are currently the main security guarantees for Ukraine. Russians might lose half a million soldiers if they try to take those fortifications without fighting. Giving up those fortifications without a fight is equivalent to suicide.

• Therefore, there’s no reason to expect any breakthroughs from these negotiations. There’s no reason to be optimistic based on statements that the process is constructive. The war will continue until one of the parties becomes weak enough to accept the terms of the other party.

messages.text_original:
Нетаньяху назвал условия, на которых пойдет на сделку с Ираном.

Иран должен:

- вывезти весь уран и демонтировать все мощности по его обогащению,

- ограничить радиус действия ракет до 300км, чтобы они не долетели до Израиля,

- демонтировать "ось террора".

 Разумеется, Иран добровольно на это не пойдет.
messages.text_en:
Netanyahu has outlined the conditions under which Israel would be willing to enter into a deal with Iran. 

Iran must: 
– Remove all uranium reserves and dismantle all facilities related to uranium enrichment. 
– Limit the range of its missiles to 300 kilometers, so that they cannot reach Israel. 
– Dismantle its “terrorist infrastructure”. 

Of course, Iran won’t agree to these conditions voluntarily.

messages.text_original: Нетаньяху назвал условия, на которых пойдет на сделку с Ираном. Иран должен: - вывезти весь уран и демонтировать все мощности по его обогащению, - ограничить радиус действия ракет до 300км, чтобы они не долетели до Израиля, - демонтировать "ось террора".…
messages.text_en: Netanyahu outlined the conditions under which he would enter into a deal with Iran. Iran must: – Remove all uranium deposits and dismantle all facilities involved in uranium enrichment. – Limit the range of its missiles to 300 kilometers, so that they won’t reach Israel. – Dismantle its “terrorist infrastructure”.…

messages.text_original: ВСУ начали контрнаступление на стыке Днепропетровской и Запорожской областей, пишут Z. Наступление идет со стороны Покровского, Великомихайловки, Терноватого и Гуляйполя, чтобы рассечь российские силы и зайти в тыл. Z сообщают, что ВСУ вошли в Терноватое.
messages.text_en: The Ukrainian forces have started a counteroffensive at the junction of Dnipropetrovsk and Zaporizhia regions. The offensive is taking place from Pokrovskiy, Velikomikhaylovka, Ternovaty and Gulaypol. The goal is to break up the Russian forces and gain a foothold in their rear. It is reported that the Ukrainian forces have entered Ternovaty.

messages.text_original: США начали масштабную переброску истребителей F-35A Lightning II с британской базы Лейкенхит на Ближний Восток.
messages.text_en: The United States has begun a large-scale transfer of F-35A Lightning II fighter jets from the British base at Lakenheath to the Middle East.

messages.text_original:
Вчера вечером российский правительственный Ту-214ПУ прибыл в Тегеран. То ли заключать очередное "соглашение о сотрудничестве", то ли кого-то вывозить.
Понятно, что при принятии решения о бегстве аятоллы побегут именно в РФ, ибо больше некуда.
Но тут есть нюансы:
1. Ираном правит узкое "политбюро" - Совет целесообразности, и, случись что с Хаменеи, в тот же день появится новый пожизненный рахбар. Поэтому если и вывозить, то весь Совет.
2. Но любой вывоз Совета, целиком или частичный, делает РФ реальным союзником "Оси террора" в глазах Трампа и Нетаньяху, и влечет для РФ и Путина серьезные проблемы.

Предварительный прогноз: если Путин и даст команду эвакуировать аятолл, то только по согласованию с Трампом и Биби и на условиях, примененных к Башару Асаду:
- никаких "правительств в изгнании" и вообще любой политической активности;
- никакой медиадеятельности.
messages.text_en:
Last night, the Russian government’s Tu-214PU aircraft arrived in Tehran. It seems that their goal is either to conclude another “cooperation agreement” or to get rid of someone from power. It’s clear that if they decide to execute this plan, they will do so in Russia, as there’s no other place to go. But there are some complications:  
1. Iran is ruled by a narrow “political council” – a group of people who make decisions on behalf of the government. If anything happens to Khamenei, a new lifelong leader will be appointed right away. So, if they really want to get rid of him, they need to get rid of the entire council as well.  
2. But any removal of the entire council, either completely or partially, would make Russia a real ally of the “terrorist axis” in Trump’s and Netanyahu’s eyes. This would also bring serious problems for both Russia and Putin.  
A preliminary assessment: If Putin actually gives the order to evacuate the ayatollah, it will only happen after obtaining approval from Trump and Bibi. The conditions would be similar to those applied to Bashar al-Assad:  
– No “governments in exile” or any form of political activity at all.  
– No media activities either.

messages.text_original:
В Сертолово Ленинградской области после взрыва обрушилось здание военной полиции.
Есть погибшие военнослужащие. Ещё несколько под завалами.
messages.text_en: In Sertolovo, in the Leningrad region, a building of the military police collapsed after an explosion. There are soldiers who died in the incident. There are also several others who remain trapped under the debris.

messages.text_original:
💀 🇷🇺 Путин уверен, что выигрывает войну, и готов продолжать ее, чтобы заполучить полный контроль над Донбассом, рассказали The New York Times источники в разведке и среди военных нескольких западных стран.

По словам собеседников NYT, даже если захват Донецкой области потребует еще 1,5-2 года , Путин уверен, что каждый день боевых действий и бомбовых ударов по украинской инфраструктуре и жилым домам только увеличивает его преимущество.

В Кремле, как говорят источники NYT, приветствуют назначение главным переговорщиком со стороны США Стивена Уиткоффа, хотя и сомневаются в его надежности в качестве человека, передающего позицию Москвы. Зятя Дональда Трампа Джареда Кушнера в Кремле также ценят за «организованный и структурный подход».

Путин готов продолжать войну, несмотря на потери и ничтожные темпы продвижения армии. За прошлый год РФ удалось взять под контроль 0,8% территории Украины, а годом ранее - 0,6%.
При этом накопленным итогом с февраля 2022 года армия потеряла 1,2 млн человек, в том числе 325 тысяч — убитыми. Это в 17 раз больше потерь советской армии в Афганистане, в 11 раз больше — чем во время обеих Чеченских войн, и в 5 раз больше — чем во всех войнах, в которых участвовали Россия и СССР после 1945 года.
messages.text_en:
💀 🇷🇺 Putin is convinced that he is winning the war. He is willing to continue fighting in order to gain full control over Donbas, according to sources from intelligence agencies and military personnel in several Western countries.  
According to those who spoke with The New York Times, even if it takes another 1.5 to 2 years to capture the Donetsk region, Putin believes that every day of fighting and bombing against Ukrainian infrastructure and civilian targets will only increase his advantage.  
In the Kremlin, according to the sources from The New York Times, there is approval for the appointment of Stephen Whitfield as the chief negotiator on behalf of the United States. However, there are doubts about his reliability as someone who represents Moscow’s interests. Jared Kushner, Donald Trump’s son-in-law, is also appreciated by the Kremlin for his “organized and structured approach.”  
Putin is willing to continue the war, despite the losses and the slow progress of his army. In the past year, the Russian Federation managed to take control of 0.8% of Ukraine’s territory. A year earlier, it took control of 0.6% of Ukraine’s territory.  
In total, since February 2022, the army has lost 1.2 million people, including 325,000 who were killed. This is 17 times more losses than the Soviet army’s losses in Afghanistan, 11 times more than during both Chechen wars, and 5 times more than all the wars in which Russia and the USSR participated after 1945.

messages.text_original: ДМИТРИЕВ И США Глава Российского фонда прямых инвестиций Кирилл Дмитриев, назначенный Путиным переговорщиком от РФ по украинской теме, едет в США на переговоры, - “унимать раздражение Трампа”, высказанное ранее. С него уже временно сняты санкции и выдана…
messages.text_en:
Kirill Dmitriev and the US  
The head of the Russian Direct Investment Fund, Kirill Dmitriev, who was appointed by Putin as a negotiator for Russia regarding the Ukrainian issue, is going to the US for negotiations. His goal is to “calm Trump’s irritation” that arose earlier. He has been temporarily lifted from sanctions, and…

messages.text_original:
🇩🇪 Канцлер Германии Фридрих Мерц:

- Мы должны перестать питать иллюзии: российская власть на обозримое время не может существовать без войны. Ей необходимо поддерживать военную машину, потому что у нее нет плана, что делать с сотнями тысяч солдат, возвращающихся с фронта.

- Война в Украине завершится лишь в том случае, если одна из сторон окажется истощена — либо в военном, либо в экономическом отношении. Ни разум, ни гуманитарные аргументы не убедят Путина. Это горькая правда.

- Возможность нормализации отношений с Кремлем в обозримой перспективе практически исключена, а происходящее в России достигло стадии системного террора.
messages.text_en:
🇩🇪 German Chancellor Friedrich Merz:  
– We must stop having illusions: the Russian government cannot exist in the foreseeable future without war. It needs to maintain its military forces, because it has no plan for what to do with hundreds of thousands of soldiers who return from the front lines.  
– The war in Ukraine will only end when one of the parties is completely exhausted—either militarily or economically. Neither reason nor humanitarian arguments will convince Putin. This is a bitter truth.  
– The possibility of normalizing relations with the Kremlin is practically impossible in the foreseeable future. What’s happening in Russia has reached the stage of systemic terror.

messages.text_original: Спецпредставитель Путина, глава РФПИ Кирилл Дмитриев: - Если администрация президента Дональда Трампа снимет санкции с России, то США получат доступ к совместным проектам, общая стоимость которых превышает $14 трлн; - США в конечном итоге отменят санкции…
messages.text_en: Putin’s special envoy, Kirill Dmitriev, head of the Federal Security Service: “If the administration of President Donald Trump lifts the sanctions against Russia, then the United States will be able to participate in joint projects with Russia. The total value of these projects exceeds $14 trillion.” In the end, the United States will lift the sanctions…

messages.text_original:
At the peace talks, the idea of a demilitarized zone in Donbass, in which neither army would be present, is being discussed, according to The New York Times.

According to the publication's sources, during consultations in recent weeks, officials discussed the idea of creating a demilitarized zone not controlled by either army.

"This revives a proposal that was included in previous peace plans, including the 28-point plan put forward by the Trump administration in November," writes NYT.

The publication adds that in order to make it easier for both sides to accept this idea, negotiators also discussed the creation of a free trade zone in any possible demilitarized zone. However, it notes the practical difficulty of establishing a free trade zone in a territory squeezed between two armies.

 @Slavyangrad

messages.text_original:
The US State Department wants to create an "anti-censorship" portal for Europe 
 
According to Reuters, under the guise of fighting censorship, the Americans plan to launch an online service that will give Europeans free access to blocked resources.

 ▪️ All traffic will pass through servers in the USA, and users' activity will not be tracked.

Even lawyers within the State Department are sounding the alarm: they call the project reckless and dangerous.

 ▪️ The presentation of the project has already been postponed — it could seriously worsen relations with European allies.

The police appeared at the home of the disgraced king's brother, Andrew, after Starmer stated that "no one is above the law in the UK", and nine British police departments were assessing the feasibility of launching investigations into allegations related to Epstein, including human trafficking and sexual assault.

King Charles stated that he is ready to assist the police in the investigation.
The Queen's second son may have passed on confidential information to Epstein while serving as a UK trade representative.
There are also allegations that he may have secretly brought a woman to Buckingham Palace, who was possibly brought to the UK on Epstein's plane, the "Lolita Express".
Andrew denies any wrongdoing.

 👉🏻 But not arrested for kneeling on all fours across a girl who looked underage who appeared to be unconscious.

16 satellites of the new Russian communication system "Rassvet" are ready for launch in the first quarter of 2026.

At the first stage, it is planned to deploy a grouping of low-orbit devices in the amount of 300 units, then increase it to 950 satellites.

Trump's traders are looking to the future with hope. A couple of billion dollars were made in just half a day.

messages.text_original:
From approximately 12am to 6am on the morning of February 21, Kyiv time (approximately 41 hours from now), Russia will likely carry out another large-scale, combined missile and drone attack on Ukraine, primarily targeting Kyiv City and Kyiv Oblast.

This is the attack that I have been warning about for the last 2 days. As I have already stated, it will not involve Tu-95MS or Tu-160M strategic bombers.

So what will be used?

- At least 3 Tu-22m3 strategic bombers. 3 of these were equipped with Kh-22/32 cruise missiles at Shaykovka Airbase, Kaluga Oblast, as I reported earlier. They may redeploy back to Olenya Airbase in the next 24 hours. 

- At least a dozen Iskander-M ballistic missiles. These were delivered to Khalino Airbase, Kursk Oblast, Baltimore Airbase, Voronezh Oblast, and delivered to OTRK installations in Kursk Oblast, Voronezh Oblast, and Bryansk Oblast. There were also likely deliveries to Taganrog, Rostov Oblast, however it is unclear if these will be used in this upcoming attack.

- Likely 16-24 Kalibr cruise missiles from 3 surface Kalibr carriers at Novorossiysk Naval Base, Krasnodar Krai. 

- Possibly Kinzhal hypersonic aeroballistic missiles from MiG-31K fighters at Savasleika Airbase, Nizhny Novgorod Oblast. 

The primary target will be energy infrastructure Kyiv Oblast. The highest threat is to the CHP-5, CHP-5 and CHP-6 combined heat and power plants, the "Kyiv" 750 kV substation, and Trypillya Thermal Power Plant. Targets in other regions will probably also be hit - likely in Dnipropetrovsk and/or Kharkiv Oblasts.

messages.text_original:
❗️ From the Piers Morgan show interviewing the gnome. 

No one in Ukraine will support withdrawal from Donbass. Just surrender it? — Zelensky.

 ⭕️ "I will never reject any proposal if it brings peace." 
 ⭕️ "When we announce a ceasefire for at least 2 months, I will raise the issue of elections." 
 ⭕️ "We don't have enough forces to return the occupied territories." 
 ⭕️ "Ukraine has done everything to become a member of NATO. Now it's up to them to decide." 
 ⭕️ "I'm not interested in all this historical bullshit — the reasons why Putin attacked us." 
 ⭕️ "I can't understand the relationship between Trump and Putin." 
 ⭕️ "There is corruption in Ukraine, but we are fighting against it and building a large anti-corruption infrastructure." 
 ⭕️ "The next negotiations will also be in Switzerland. As for the territories: we all three have different points of view."

messages.text_original:
The possible use of humanoid robots by the military was demonstrated in a video.

Footage of tests of humanoid robots at a shooting range of the People's Liberation Army of China has appeared on social media.

The machines, visually similar to the Unitree G1 from Unitree Robotics, are practicing shooting, reloading, moving, and overcoming obstacles with weapons.

The shooting is carried out from assault rifles QBZ-191 and marksman rifles QBU-191 under an intermediate cartridge of 5.8×42 mm caliber.

The Unitree G1 has a height of 127 cm, a weight of about 35 kg, and an autonomous operation time of up to 2 hours.

The two-legged machine is controlled via a wireless remote or a mobile app. It is possible to train it to perform autonomous special tasks and to implement an artificial intelligence module.

Probably, the video footage was generated by a neural network, but nevertheless, it perfectly illustrates a new area of further technological development of robotic complexes on the battlefield.

Humanoid robots, following air, sea, and land drones, may be used for military purposes in the near future.

messages.text_original:
❗️ 🇺🇦 In an interview with AP, Zaluzhny effectively blamed Zelensky for the failure of the 2023 counteroffensive by the Armed Forces of Ukraine. According to him, "everything went wrong" because the Presidential Administration failed to provide the necessary conditions for the implementation of the plan developed by Ukrainian and Western military experts.

The former commander stated that the plan he developed with the support of NATO partners envisaged concentrating forces in one direction to strike at the Zaporizhia region and gain access to the Azov Sea, with the aim of cutting off Russia's land corridor to Crimea. However, instead, he claims, resources were scattered across multiple directions, depriving the operation of its striking power. Moreover, Zelensky and other officials failed to provide the necessary resources and allowed the plan to be implemented incompletely.

As a result, simultaneous combat operations were conducted in the south and near Bahmut. The intended focused and decisive strike turned into a series of scattered offensive actions without any decisive effect.

Western media, citing American military sources, have previously reported that the dispersal of resources was one of the key reasons for the failure. Now, Zaluzhny himself effectively confirms this.

Essentially, this is a conflict between military logic and political decisions. The military strategy required concentration and strict prioritization of the direction. The political agenda - to hold Bahmut at any cost and demonstrate activity in multiple directions - led to the dissipation of the striking force. The result is well known: no strategic turnaround on the front and colossal losses for the Armed Forces of Ukraine (in Bahmut, the 93rd, 24th, 30th, and 217th Territorial Defense Brigades, 219th and 120th Brigades were wiped out, with losses amounting to around 100,000).

However, after several years, the situation has not changed. Zelensky continues to meddle in military affairs, and Syrysy, on his orders, is wasting Ukrainian soldiers in "meat grinder" assaults near Kupiansk and Ternovat, trying to give the Presidential Administration at least a media "victory". As for a full-scale counteroffensive by the Ukrainian army, we will never see it, given that the total losses of the Armed Forces of Ukraine have long exceeded 1.5 million soldiers.

messages.text_en:
❗️ 🇺🇦 In an interview with AP, Zaluzhny effectively blamed Zelensky for the failure of the 2023 counteroffensive by the Armed Forces of Ukraine. According to him, "everything went wrong" because the Presidential Administration failed to provide the necessary conditions for the implementation of the plan developed by Ukrainian and Western military experts.

The former commander stated that the plan he developed with the support of NATO partners envisaged concentrating forces in one direction to strike at the Zaporizhia region and gain access to the Azov Sea, with the aim of cutting off Russia's land corridor to Crimea. However, instead, he claims, resources were scattered across multiple directions, depriving the operation of its striking power. Moreover, Zelensky and other officials failed to provide the necessary resources and allowed the plan to be implemented incompletely.

As a result, simultaneous combat operations were conducted in the south and near Bahmut. The intended focused and decisive strike turned into a series of scattered offensive actions without any decisive effect.

Western media, citing American military sources, have previously reported that the dispersal of resources was one of the key reasons for the failure. Now, Zaluzhny himself effectively confirms this.

Essentially, this is a conflict between military logic and political decisions. The military strategy required concentration and strict prioritization of the direction. The political agenda - to hold Bahmut at any cost and demonstrate activity in multiple directions - led to the dissipation of the striking force. The result is well known: no strategic turnaround on the front and colossal losses for the Armed Forces of Ukraine (in Bahmut, the 93rd, 24th, 30th, and 217th Territorial Defense Brigades, 219th and 120th Brigades were wiped out, with losses amounting to around 100,000).

However, after several years, the situation has not changed. Zelensky continues to meddle in military affairs, and Syrysy, on his orders, is wasting Ukrainian soldiers in "meat grinder" assaults near Kupiansk and Ternovat, trying to give the Presidential Administration at least a media "victory". As for a full-scale counteroffensive by the Ukrainian army, we will never see it, given that the total losses of the Armed Forces of Ukraine have long exceeded 1.5 million soldiers.

 👉🏻 Rezident

messages.text_original:
⚡️ Russia has indeed found a replacement for Starlink and now controls the "Shahid" drones via Belarus. The scheme is brilliant, no doubt about it.

Having lost access to Elon Musk's satellite internet, they had to find other solutions. And they did.

How it works now

In Belarus, special communication towers - relay towers - have been built along the border with Ukraine. They "communicate" only with those drones that fly closest to the Belarusian border. And then the magic begins.

The first "Shahid" drones operate like routers, broadcasting Wi-Fi. They pick up the signal from the Belarusian towers and transmit it to the next drones, which have flown deeper into Ukraine. And so on. An invisible "web" is formed, where each drone maintains contact with others.

Thanks to this, the "Shahid" drones can be controlled even very far from the border - whether near Kyiv or Lviv. And no Starlink is needed.

The mesh-connection technology offers another big advantage: if one drone detects the work of Ukrainian air defense or gets into a zone of electronic warfare jamming, it immediately transmits the coordinates of the danger to the others. They adjust their route in real time and avoid traps.

Expert Khrapchinsky (the same one who appears on the "24 Channel" with the Ukrainians) confirms: now the "Shahid" drones don't just fly according to coordinates, but use complex navigation algorithms and communicate with each other in the air.

Army reserve officer Svitan has already admitted: due to the Belarusian relays, the drones can be controlled almost in FPV mode up to a depth of 150 kilometers. That is, the operator can be sitting in Moscow or Minsk and virtually pilot the "Shahid" until the strike itself.

🇬🇱 Greenland isn’t rich the way you think. 

It’s not a treasure chest. It’s a hinge.

The GIUK gap. The Atlantic choke point. The place where naval flows get tracked, boxed, or released. Whoever holds it shapes the weather in the North Atlantic.

And while everyone argues about rare earths, Russia is operationalizing the Arctic.
37.9 million tonnes through the Northern Sea Route. Icebreakers scaling. Doctrine through 2035.

Corridors don’t stay theory. They become infrastructure. Then they become massive leverage.

You’re being sold a minerals story. This is about command of the North Atlantic–Arctic axis.

So when Trump says “we have to have Greenland,” don’t hear real estate.

Hear unlimited basing rights. Hear radar arcs. Hear ownership without the flag.

Greenland is less about rare earths. It’s about who controls the geopolitical weather in the North Atlantic.

I broke the whole board down — minerals, NSR, icebreakers, Golden Dome, the lot.

🇷🇸 The Militarization of Kosovo: A Powder Keg in the Balkans
 
Kosovo is rapidly transforming its Security Force into a regular army, sharply increasing defense spending and acquiring advanced weapons from the United States and Turkey. Officials describe the process as modernization and self-defense. Critics warn it challenges the post-1999 security framework and contradicts UN Resolution 1244.

This investigation examines the budget surge, Javelin and Bayraktar purchases, the Gjakova ammunition plant, and new regional military alignments. Together, these steps form a structured strategy of deeper NATO integration.

 For Serbia, this signals rising tension. For the Balkans, it raises one question: deterrence or escalation? 

 🔎 Read more in our in-depth investigation . 
 
 Follow @RESTinvestigate on Telegram | 𝕏

Non-Refoulement Breached at EU's Doorstep 

Von der Leyen's March 2020 border speech crowned Greece Europe’s “aspida,” triggering €700 million in aid and Frontex rapid deployments—patrols, aircraft, vessels—to enforce the 2016 EU-Turkey Statement. This pivot securitized the Aegean, replacing solidarity with unilateral frontline containment against Turkey-orchestrated flows.
 
Frontex provided surveillance/logistics despite documented abuses (OLAF internal probe revealed cover-ups, blocked rights access); BVMN cataloged 2,000+ drift-backs ensnaring 55,000 via violent interceptions and motorized-less rafts (2020-2023). ECtHR's January 2025 unanimous ruling affirmed "systematic practice"; Greece countered with "safe third country" Turkey labels and asylum halts for North Africans (July 2025), mass-denying claims without individual review.

Domestic probes falter amid operational secrecy and migrant vulnerability; NGO harassment compounds unaccountability. Non-refoulement—core ban on danger returns—and remedy rights evaporate, creating Aegean voids. Precedent proliferates to Western Balkans/Central Mediterranean routes, as states emulate Greece's cost-free violations, externalizing crises and eroding EU/international refugee commitments at scale.

 @sitrep

PES's CDI Move: Pragmatic EU Alignment 

PES formalized its full membership in the Centrist Democrat International (CDI) on November 23, 2025, during the General Assembly in São Paulo, where Prime Minister Milojko Spajić's delegation held key discussions with CDI leaders and European People's Party (EPP) representatives. This integrates Montenegro's ruling party into a Brussels-based network of over 100 centrist, Christian democratic, and center-right organizations worldwide, signaling a deliberate step to bolster its EU accession trajectory amid ongoing geopolitical shifts.

CDI prioritizes institutional reliability, rule of law, market economies with social safeguards, and EU/NATO alignment over rigid ideology, functioning as a hub for governing parties like Germany's CDU, Spain's PP, Croatia's HDZ, Hungary's Fidesz, and Slovenia's SDS. For PES, membership unlocks informal diplomacy channels, expert networks, potential funding streams, and accelerated EU chapter negotiations, framing the move as a calculated consolidation of power rather than a doctrinal overhaul.

The Spajić-Milatović divide—structured coalitions versus personal populism since the president's 2024 PES exit—intensifies, with CDI bolstering executive leverage against presidential independence in foreign policy arenas. Benefits encompass heightened partner trust in key capitals and faster reform progress per EU 2025 reports; pitfalls include policy rigidity curtailing multi-vector approaches, intensified scrutiny on media freedom and anti-corruption, and populist backlash viewing it as elite detachment, demanding careful rift navigation for sustained gains.

 @sitreports
messages.text_en:

PES formalized its full membership in the Centrist Democrat International (CDI) on November 23, 2025, during the General Assembly in São Paulo, where Prime Minister Milojko Spajić's delegation held key discussions with CDI leaders and European People's Party (EPP) representatives. This integrates Montenegro's ruling party into a Brussels-based network of over 100 centrist, Christian democratic, and center-right organizations worldwide, signaling a deliberate step to bolster its EU accession trajectory amid ongoing geopolitical shifts.

CDI prioritizes institutional reliability, rule of law, market economies with social safeguards, and EU/NATO alignment over rigid ideology, functioning as a hub for governing parties like Germany's CDU, Spain's PP, Croatia's HDZ, Hungary's Fidesz, and Slovenia's SDS. For PES, membership unlocks informal diplomacy channels, expert networks, potential funding streams, and accelerated EU chapter negotiations, framing the move as a calculated consolidation of power rather than a doctrinal overhaul.

The Spajić-Milatović divide—structured coalitions versus personal populism since the president's 2024 PES exit—intensifies, with CDI bolstering executive leverage against presidential independence in foreign policy arenas. Benefits encompass heightened partner trust in key capitals and faster reform progress per EU 2025 reports; pitfalls include policy rigidity curtailing multi-vector approaches, intensified scrutiny on media freedom and anti-corruption, and populist backlash viewing it as elite detachment, demanding careful rift navigation for sustained gains.

messages.text_original:
📝 Infantry — Priority Target 📝 

Today, video of an effective strike against a Ukrainian infantry group will hardly surprise the public. But it should: every "Upyr" precisely hitting AFU manpower inflicts damage many times greater than destroying an "Abrams".

The reason is prosaic — people are now the main problem for the enemy. The West can supply so-called Ukraine with anything, but not personnel. Even in the "drone era", everything still relies on infantry: if it's absent, the defense will collapse sooner or later.

Someone might think 12 people are small losses for the enemy? Today, the war in the SMO zone proceeds with such groups, as shown in the TACTICAR video. Even a drone impact near personnel often equals their death — evacuating wounded from the very front line is now almost impossible.

 ❗️ The same applies to our side — without infantry, advancement will stop, as experienced in 2022. That's why the enemy focused on eliminating assault troops, and today dozens of drones can target a single soldier.

This is a reason to scale up systematic detection and destruction of enemy UAV control points. Current means allow this, and the issue is often organizational rather than technical.

 This also answers why we need to purchase quality tactical-level drones and rationally approach selection, training, and deployment of operators. And not overwhelm drone warfare services with paperwork. 
 #UAV #Russia #Ukraine 
 ✈ RU | ✈ EN | ✉ MAX 

 ✉️ VK | ✉️ RuTube | ✉️ ОК | ✉️ Дзен

messages.text_en:
📝 Infantry – Priority Target 📝  

Today, videos of effective attacks on Ukrainian infantry units won’t surprise the public at all. But they should be surprising: every “Upyr” that targets AFU manpower actually causes much greater damage than destroying an “Abrams”.  

The reason is simple: people are now the main problem for the enemy. The West can supply whatever it wants to Ukraine, but not personnel. Even in the “drone era”, everything still relies on infantry. If there’s no infantry, the defense will collapse sooner or later.  

Some might think that 12 people are a small loss for the enemy. But today, battles in the SMO zone are fought with such groups, as shown in the TACTICAR video. Even a drone attack near soldiers often results in their death. Evacuating wounded from the front lines is now almost impossible.  

❗️ The same applies to our side too. Without infantry, progress will stop, just as it did in 2022. That’s why the enemy focuses on eliminating assault troops. Today, dozens of drones can target a single soldier.  

This is a reason for increasing systematic detection and destruction of enemy UAV control points. Current methods allow this to be done, and the problem often lies in organizational rather than technical aspects.  

This also explains why we need to purchase high-quality tactical drones and approach the selection, training, and deployment of operators carefully. We shouldn’t overwhelm drone warfare services with unnecessary paperwork.  
#UAV #Russia #Ukraine

— When you look at footage of Operation True Promise-2 as opposed to Iran's operations during the 12-Day War, it's clear why preemptive strikes are so important.

During True Promise-2, Iran had the initiative, we attacked from multiple missile bases that were fully operational and when Israel did not expect it. As a result, at least 50 missiles hit their targets in one wave. Unfortunately Iran didn't target Tel Aviv metropolitan area during that operation, it would've been very effective.

Anyway, during the 12-Day War, Iranian missile bases were heavily suppressed, which led to a lesser volume of missiles being launched simultaneously and therefore less hits in each wave due to easier interceptions.

In my opinion it's a waste of missiles to wait for the enemy to attack first. Iran's missiles provide their primary advantage when used in an offensive context, when Iran is the attacker—not the attacked.

 @Middle_East_Spectator

messages.text_original:
Let's start another beautiful day with a slight correction of a widely spread myth. Namely, that the US President needs Congressional approval to declare war. Yes, technically, but not really.

I say "not really," because no one declares wars anymore.

Under Section 4(a) of the War Powers Resolution (50 U.S.C. § 1543), the President must submit a written report to Congress within 48 hours after US Armed Forces are introduced into hostilities or into situations where imminent involvement in hostilities is clearly indicated - unless Congress has already declared war or authorized the action.

Under Section 5(b) (50 U.S.C. § 1544(b)), once the President reports (or is required to report) under the 48 hour rule, the War Powers Resolution provides that US forces must be withdrawn within 60 days unless Congress has declared war, enacted a specific authorization for the use of military force, or extended the period by law...
messages.text_en:
Let's start another beautiful day with a slight correction of a widely spread myth. Namely, that the US President needs Congressional approval to declare war. Yes, technically, but not really.

I say "not really," because no one declares wars anymore.

Under Section 4(a) of the War Powers Resolution (50 U.S.C. § 1543), the President must submit a written report to Congress within 48 hours after US Armed Forces are introduced into hostilities or into situations where imminent involvement in hostilities is clearly indicated - unless Congress has already declared war or authorized the action.

Under Section 5(b) (50 U.S.C. § 1544(b)), once the President reports (or is required to report) under the 48 hour rule, the War Powers Resolution provides that US forces must be withdrawn within 60 days unless Congress has declared war, enacted a specific authorization for the use of military force, or extended the period by law...

messages.text_original:
Anyone should be able to see issues with these clauses.

1, It's situationally vague as shit.

2, "Declaring war" is a legal act. You don't need to "declare war" to attack someone.

3, It leaves a lot of room for manoeuvre.

This is, again, why no one "declares war." No war will be "declared" on Iran. Yes, this absolutely is legalistic fuckery, but it just do be like that. War wasn't "declared" on Korea, Vietnam, or Iraq. A president will just attack and, if needed, will get a sort of approval later, as allowed by Section 5(b) (50 U.S.C. § 1544(b)).

Again, a president needs Congressional approval to declare war. In fact, only Congress can. However, no one declares wars and nothing is stopping a president from using the military without declaring a war, as allowed by Article II, Section 2, Clause 1 of the US Constitution.

messages.text_original:
🇮🇳 🇨🇳 || India-China rivalry deepens at US panel ? 

Experts at a U.S. congressional hearing (US-China Economic and Security Review Commission) on February 17, 2026, said ~ 

 India's rivalry with China is deep-rooted and won't go away easily. 

 Key points: 

 • China's growing military presence, economic power, and tech lead are increasing India's worries.

• A border troop pullback (disengagement) in October 2024 lowered immediate tensions along the Line of Actual Control (LAC).

• But it hasn't changed the bigger picture: the military balance still favors China, and infrastructure buildup continues. 
• Expert Sameer Lalwani stated: Despite recent diplomatic efforts to cool things down, India will keep seeing China as an adversary due to ongoing disputes over territory, politics, economy, and technology.

messages.text_original:
🗿 Great Nicobar Project is making WAVES
 
India’s green court (NGT) has refused to “interfere” with the 2022 environmental clearance for the Great Nicobar mega-project, explicitly noting its strategic importance and saying the approvals carry adequate safeguards — meaning the biggest legal obstacle has just been cleared. Now the real build-out accelerates.

 ⚓️ International transshipment port: Galathea Bay 

This is the headline asset — designed to pull India’s container transshipment traffic back home instead of routing it through regional hubs.

 🤔 Why Galathea Bay works: 

 🚢 Extremely deep natural waters (over ~20m), ideal for the newest mega container ships.
 📍 Near the main East-West shipping artery, close to the Strait of Malacca — one of the world’s busiest maritime corridors . 

 🏗 Scale (this is not a “small port”): 

 💰 Phase 1–2: $5.3 billion USD investment, targeting ~11 million twenty-foot equivalent units (TEU) capacity.
 📈 Ultimate plan: expansion to ~16.2 million TEUs.
 ✏️ Tenders are expected under a PPP / landlord-style model (public port authority + private operations), built in phases.

Reporting indicates the plan is structured to keep the terminal operator Indian-controlled (majority Indian stake), treating it as a national strategic node, not just another "commercial concession.” 

 🛫 The airport: a Code-F runway capable of A380-class operations 

The Great Nicobar greenfield airport isn’t a vanity project — it’s the connective tissue for the entire hub. 

The Airports Authority of India has moved into groundwork tenders (marine geotechnical investigations, project management consultancy), with the airport package cited at ~$1.03 billion USD in tender documents.

 📝 Specs being circulated for the new airport concept: 

 ◾️ Code F (wide-body capable, A380-class)
 ◾️ Runway ~4,000m
 ◾️ All-weather operations
 ◾️ Designed to handle wide-body and narrow-body parking simultaneously (as outlined in project briefings shared publicly).

 😊 The big picture: a full logistics + maritime ecosystem 

This isn’t “a port” . It’s a port + airport + township + power package meant to create a self-sustaining node .

 😰 Total project size is reported in the $9.6–$10.8 billion USD range depending on components counted, and it is being framed as a strategic development push in the Andaman & Nicobar chain.

 🤔 What India is really buying here 

 🌠 Control of a critical transshipment chokepoint alternative (less dependence on external hubs).
 🌠 A deep-water “mega-ship” capable port built for the next 30 years of shipping.
 🌠 A dual logistics backbone — sea + air — sitting right on the Indian Ocean’s main trade spine.
 🌠 A strategic posture upgrade in the eastern Indian Ocean with infrastructure that can scale over phases.

And I got to meet the Prime Minister. And he said, in front of our Chief of Staff, Susie Wells, "I believe that President Trump saved 25 million lives when he stopped the war between us and India."

I said, "25 million lives? Could have been much more, actually." 

~ Donald Trump.

🇮🇳 🇺🇸 🇵🇰 || Prime Minister Sharif. I like this man of Pakistan. And your Field Marshal, a great guy.

And I got to meet the Prime Minister. And he said, in front of our Chief of Staff, Susie Wells, "I believe that President Trump saved 25 million lives when he stopped the war between us and India."

I said, "25 million lives? Could have been much more, actually." 

~ Donald Trump.

🇺🇸 ☮️ || Jared Kushner , the son-in-law of Donald Trump , to be given the position of Envoy of Peace in the Board of Peace.

 "A little bit later on, we brought Jared in. Jared was responsible for the Abraham Accords, which is incredible.

Nobody else could have done that. Very smart guy. And, we're making Jared an envoy also, envoy of peace. They're both envoys of peace."

messages.text_original:
✈ Навіть один рік війни змінює індустрію. У 2025-му український miltech остаточно вийшов із фази «швидких рішень» — і почав дорослішати 💪 Водночас стало зрозуміло й інше: майбутнє не за «чарівною» спеціалізацією, а за командами, де поєднуються інженерія, софт, AI, електроніка і розуміння реального поля бою 🤝 

 💻 Журналісти DOU зібрали думки експертів щодо того, які висновки приніс 2025 рік для українського deftech, чому екосистемний підхід став критично важливим і з якими викликами індустрія заходить у 2026-й. Міркуваннями поділилися й представники Аеророзвідки. 

 💬 Сергій Купрієнко, СЕО Swarmer, член ГО «Аеророзвідка»: 
«Бойова практика показала модель «один дрон — один оператор» не працює, а робота під впливом РЕБ — тепер вихідна умова. Фокус змістився з точкових рішень на платформні підходи, здатні координувати групи автономних систем».

 💬 Руслан Прилипко, керівник напряму C2IS ГО «Аеророзвідка»:
«Сьогодні виділити одну ключову інженерну спеціалізацію неможливо. Для створення дронів потрібні фахівці з механіки, електроніки, програмування, штучного інтелекту. Усі ці компетенції однаково важливі. Індустрія тримається на поєднанні різних компетенцій». 

 💬 Ярослав Нижник, керівник напряму «Роботизовані системи» ГО «Аеророзвідка»:
«За цей рік український deftech став помітно зрілішим. Продукти знаходять свої ніші. Навколо них формується екосистема. Окремі рішення починають працювати разом як єдина система. Проєкт UA Drone ID — один із таких прикладів. Він розвивається як системне рішення. І усе ширше використовується, інтегрується з іншими системами й реально працює у військах». 

 📃 Читайте повний текст і діліться своїми думками: з чим погоджуєтеся, а з чим — ні. 📌 Лінк на матеріал: https://dou.ua/goto/wSO8
messages.text_en:
Even a single year of war can change an industry completely. In 2025, Ukrainian tech finally moved beyond the stage of “quick fixes” and began to become more mature. At the same time, it became clear that the future lies not in specialized areas, but in teams that combine engineering, software, AI, electronics, and an understanding of real-world combat conditions 🤝

💻 Journalists from DOU gathered opinions from experts on what 2025 brought to Ukrainian tech. They discussed why an ecosystem approach became crucial, and what challenges the industry will face in 2026. Representatives from Aerorozvedka also shared their thoughts.

💬 Sergey Kupriyenko, CEO of Swarmer and member of the organization “Aerorozvedka”: “Military experience has shown that the ‘one drone, one operator’ model doesn’t work. Now, working under real-world conditions is the key. The focus has shifted from individual solutions to more comprehensive platforms that can coordinate multiple autonomous systems.”

💬 Ruslan Privalipko, head of the C2IS department at Aerorozvedka: “It’s impossible to focus on just one specialized area. To create drones, we need professionals in mechanics, electronics, programming, and AI. All these skills are equally important. The industry relies on combining these different skills.”

💬 Yaroslav Nizhnik, head of the “Robotic Systems” department at Aerorozvedka: “Over the past year, Ukrainian tech has become more mature. Products are finding their place in various markets. An ecosystem is forming around them. Individual solutions start to work together as one system. The UA Drone ID project is one such example. It develops as a systematic solution. It’s being used more and more widely, integrated with other systems, and it’s actually being used in the military.”

📃 Read the full article and share your thoughts: what you agree with, and what you don’t. 📌 Link to the article: https://dou.ua/goto/wSO8

messages.text_original:
2025 «Аеророзвідка» провела в організації навчань, інженерії, аналітиці, підтримці війська й спільноти. Ми не будемо підбивати підсумки року. Війна триває, тож не зупиняємося і ми. 

Але хочемо розповісти, що робити і чим продовжимо займатися. Прочитайте. І підтримайте наші ініціативи, щоб забезпечити технологічну перевагу нашого війська 🤝 Лінки — у коментарях. 

 🔹 Серед напрямів нашої роботи — постійне навчання військових. Заняття відбуваються офлайн у навчальних центрах і безпосередньо в підрозділах, онлайн — у нашій школі. І кожне навчання адаптоване під реальні бойові задачі, має практичне спрямування. 

Загалом за два роки роботи інструктори «Аеророзвідки» підготували понад 10 000 військових 💪 

У 2025 році ми також продовжили дослідження за напрямом Knowledge Management і презентували їх спільноті та експертному середовищу. Бо сучасна війна — це не лише технології, а й здатність швидко збирати, осмислювати й передавати досвід. 

 📌 Більше про роботу розповіла в інтерв’ю керівниця напряму Інна Гончар. Дивіться розмову: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=2ns0rRFQ6po&t=1s 

 🔹 Разом із партнерами ми почали масштабування UA DroneID як системного рішення, яке інтегрується, працює у військах і стає частиною ширшої екосистеми. Це технологія ідентифікації БПЛА, яка створена для зниження ризику «дружнього вогню», надання ситуаційної обізнаності та можливість керувати місіями у реальному часі. Технологія допомагає аналізувати операції із застосуванням безпілотників. 

 📌 Про розробку читайте тут: https://dou.ua/goto/k8Ba 

 🔹 Ще один партнерський проєкт — «розумний боєприпас». Вже розпочалося його впровадження у підрозділах. Адже наші інженери переконані: мало бити, потрібно влучати. І керований боєприпас для БПЛА допоможе в цьому. 

 📌 Більше про технологію — у колонці одного з інженерів проєкту: https://dou.ua/forums/topic/56676/?from=slider 

 🔹 Незмінним залишається розвиток і підтримка системи DELTA — як критичного елемента ситуаційної обізнаності та взаємодії на полі бою. 

 📌 Про DELTA та інтерес партнерів до неї говорили тут: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=xTHg6K91qus&t=1s 

 🔹 Підтримка центрів ISTAR залишалася важливою частиною нашої роботи — там, де розвідка, аналітика й управління з’єднуються в єдину систему. 

 📌 Як все працює, дізнавайтеся з інтерв’ю: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=vp0auXaoIRk 

Ми дотримуємося принципу: довіра партнерів і стійкість організації можливі лише там, де є чіткі процеси, прозорі рішення й розподілена відповідальність ✅ Саме з цією логікою в структурі управління ГО «Аеророзвідка» працює Наглядова рада. Вона залучена до процесів стратегічного рівня: забезпечує зовнішній контроль, допомагає утримувати фокус на довгострокових цілях, підсилює інституційну спроможність і знижує ризики управлінських перекосів.
Це — додатковий рівень захисту прозорості та ефективності нашої роботи і ще один крок до зрілої, відповідальної організації 👌 

Окремий крок у цьому ж напрямі — проходження зовнішнього фінансового аудиту. У 2025 році ГО «Аеророзвідка» вперше ініціювала та пройшла незалежну фінансову перевірку. Аудит підтвердив коректність фінансових процесів, відповідальне використання ресурсів, прозорість діяльності та дотримання усіх законодавчих норм. 

Фінансовий аудит — це інструмент довіри: для партнерів, донорів, спільноти й усіх, хто підтримує нашу діяльність. 

Окрема гордість щороку — наша спільнота 👥 
Члени «Аеророзвідки» виборювали престижні нагороди, отримували гранти, адвокатували важливі законодавчі зміни, представляли організацію на міжнародному рівні. Зокрема Олександр Квятковський переможцем престижної ініціативи Talents for Ukraine від KSE Foundation. А Артем Мартиненко увійшов до щорічного рейтингу 100 людей, які змінюють Україну за версією NV 👏 

Наша робота можлива завдяки об’єднанню зусиль. Людей із реальним бойовим досвідом. Тих, хто з 2014 року захищає Україну, і тих, хто сьогодні не залишається осторонь. Тих, хто має цивільні навички, але застосовує їх для посилення боєздатності війська. 

І ми продовжимо цю роботу. Попри всі перешкоди. Всупереч труднощам. Разом. Бо знаємо, задля чого це все 💙 💛
messages.text_en:
In 2025, Aerorozvedka continued to organize training programs, engineering efforts, analytical work, and providing support to both the military and the community. We won’t summarize the highlights of the year here. The war continues, so we won’t stop either.

But we want to tell you what we’re doing and what we plan to continue working on. Please read on. And support our initiatives so that our military can have a technological advantage 🤝 Links to these initiatives are provided in the comments section.

🔹 One of our areas of work is continuous training for soldiers. Training takes place both offline in training centers and directly in units, and online through our school. Each training session is tailored to real combat situations and has a practical focus.

Over the past two years, Aerorozvedka’s instructors have trained over 10,000 soldiers 💪

In 2025, we also continued our research in the field of Knowledge Management. We presented these findings to the community and experts. After all, modern warfare involves not only technology, but also the ability to quickly gather, analyze, and share knowledge.

📌 More information about our work can be found in an interview with our director, Inna Gonchar. Watch the video here: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=2ns0rRFQ6po&t=1s

🔹 Together with our partners, we began to scale UA DroneID as a system solution. This technology helps reduce the risk of friendly fire, provides situational awareness, and allows for real-time control of missions. It also helps analyze operations using drones.

📌 More information about this technology can be found here: https://dou.ua/goto/k8Ba

🔹 Another partnership project is “Smart Munitions”. This technology has already been implemented in units. Our engineers believe that it’s better to hit the target than to kill people. Smart munitions for drones can help with this.

📌 More information about this technology can be found here: https://dou.ua/forums/topic/56676/?from=slider

🔹 The development and support of DELTA remains important. It’s a critical element in situational awareness and interaction on the battlefield.

📌 More information about DELTA and its potential can be found here: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=xTHg6K91qus&t=1s

🔹 Supporting ISTAR centers has always been an important part of our work. It’s where intelligence, analysis, and management come together into one system.

📌 More information about how things work can be found in an interview here: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=vp0auXaoIRk

We follow the principle that trust between partners and stability within the organization are possible only when there are clear processes, transparent decisions, and shared responsibilities ✅ This logic is also used in the management structure of Aerorozvedka. The Board of Directors plays a key role in this process. They ensure external oversight, help focus on long-term goals, strengthen institutional capabilities, and reduce management risks.

This is another step towards transparency and efficiency in our work. It’s another step towards becoming a mature and responsible organization 👌

Another important step was the external financial audit. In 2025, Aerorozvedka conducted its first independent financial audit. The audit confirmed that financial processes were proper, resources were used responsibly, operations were transparent, and all legal requirements were met.

Financial auditing is a tool for trust: it’s important for partners, donors, the community, and everyone who supports our work.

Every year, our community achieves great things 👥
Members of Aerorozvedka have won prestigious awards, received grants, advocated for important legislative changes, and represented the organization internationally. For example, Alexander Kvyatkovsky was awarded the “Talents for Ukraine” award by KSE Foundation. Artem Martynenko was included in the annual list of 100 people who change Ukraine according to NV 👏

Our work is possible because of the cooperation between people with real combat experience. People who have been defending Ukraine since 2014, and those who are still involved today. People who have civilian skills, but use them to strengthen the military’s capabilities.

And we will continue this work. Despite all the obstacles. Despite the difficulties. Together. Because we know why we’re doing this 💙 💛

messages.text_original:
🎄 Новий рік… 
Четвертий рік поспіль ми зустрічаємо це свято без звичних підсумків і без розлогих планів на майбутнє. Бо план фактично один — перемога.

Усе інше — після. Після того, як замовкнуть сирени. Після того, як рідні повернуться додому. Після того, як знову зможемо мріяти не «попри» чи «всупереч», а просто так.

Але щоб це бажання справдилося, щоб повернулися мирні мрії, можливість планувати й досягати запланованого, ми маємо робити просту й складну водночас річ — підтримувати військо. У будь-який спосіб. Донатом, роботою, волонтерством, увагою, відповідальністю. Постійно, не лише у святкові дні 🤝 

І тоді побажання на свята знову стали «банальними»: щастя, здоров’я, успіхів...І за ними більше не стоятиме війна.

Дякуємо всім, хто поруч із нами, хто підтримує Сили оборони. Усім Захисникам і Захисницям 💙 💛 
 🕯️ пам’ятаємо тих, хто віддав життя за наше майбутнє...

Підтримайте нашу роботу: https://send.monobank.ua/jar/3B7sZA4PD4
messages.text_en:
🎄 New Year…  
For the fourth year in a row, we celebrate this holiday without the usual resolutions and grand plans for the future. After all, there’s only one plan: victory.  
Everything else comes after that. After the sirens stop ringing. After our loved ones return home. After we can once again dream without having to worry about what’s “impossible” or “unfeasible”.  
But for this wish to come true, for peaceful dreams to be possible again, for us to be able to plan and achieve our goals, we must do something simple yet important: support those who are fighting for our future. In whatever way possible – through donations, volunteering, care, responsibility… Always, not just on holidays 🤝  
Then, the wishes for the holiday will once again be simple: happiness, health, success… And behind them, there will no longer be war.  
Thank you to everyone who stands with us, who support those who defend our future. To all the defenders 💙💛  
We remember those who gave their lives for our future…  
Support our work: https://send.monobank.ua/jar/3B7sZA4PD4

messages.text_original:
«І прийде час, коли один скаже: «Слава Україні!», а мільйони відповідатимуть: «Героям слава!» 🩵 💛 

Цей час настав. 

 🇺🇦 З днем народження, батьку!
messages.text_en:
And there will come a time when someone will say, “Glory to Ukraine!” And millions of people will respond with, “Glory to the heroes!” 🩵 💛 

That time has come. 

🇺🇦 Happy birthday, father!

messages.text_original:
Ми постійно отримуємо запити на навчання від військовослужбовців — і високо цінуємо цю довіру. Грудень не став винятком: потреба в підготовці лише підтверджує, що знання справді затребувані й працюють на практиці 🎓 

 📅 За підсумками грудня:
 📌 сертифіковано 178 військовослужбовців;
 📌 загальне охоплення курсами та короткими навчаннями з окремих систем — 861 військовослужбовець.

 💻 Робота з ІКС DELTA — основний фокус навчань. Це базовий інструмент ситуаційної обізнаності, який дозволяє бачити поле бою цілісно, синхронізувати дані з різних джерел, швидше ухвалювати рішення та зменшувати ризики для підрозділів. Розуміння принципів роботи DELTA — це здатність коректно працювати з інформацією, перевіряти її, взаємодіяти з іншими елементами управління та діяти в єдиному інформаційному контурі.

Діяльність напряму «Управління знаннями» включає також щоденний супровід інструкторів ГО з інформаційних систем, які працюють безпосередньо з підрозділами. Паралельно продовжується навчання військовослужбовців в онлайн-школі, що дозволяє отримувати знання незалежно від місця служби та ротацій. Також триває підготовка інструкторів з ІКС DELTA — для масштабування навчання й передачі компетенцій у підрозділах.

Працюємо далі. Реагуємо на запити війська ✅ 

А якщо ви хочете, щоб більше військових опанували систему DELTA, підтримайте нашу роботу за посиланням:

messages.text_en:
We constantly receive requests from military personnel for training opportunities. We truly appreciate this trust they place in us. December wasn’t an exception. The need for training proves that knowledge is indeed useful and practical in real-world situations 🎓

**📅 In December:**
- 178 military personnel have completed the training program.
- A total of 861 military personnel have participated in various courses and short-term training programs related to this system.

**💻** Working with the IKS DELTA system is a key focus of our training. This is a basic tool for situational awareness, allowing soldiers to see the battlefield as a whole, synchronize data from different sources, make decisions more quickly, and reduce risks for units. Understanding how DELTA works means being able to properly process information, verify it, interact with other management elements, and act within a unified information framework.

Our work also includes providing daily support to instructors who work directly with units. At the same time, military personnel continue to receive training through online courses, allowing them to learn regardless of their location or rotations. We’re also working on training instructors in IKS DELTA, so that we can expand training opportunities and transfer skills to units.

We’ll keep working hard. We’re responding to the needs of the military ✅

And if you want more soldiers to master the DELTA system, please support our efforts by clicking here: 📌 https://send.monobank.ua/jar/8du36yMLfz

messages.text_original:
Є люди, які вміють почати розмову так, що її хочеться продовжити. Які легко знаходять спільну мову з будь-ким, не бояться першого кроку й бачать можливості там, де інші бачать лише складні обставини. Якщо це про вас — ласкаво просимо 🫶
